Farn Polrrrcar- PRACTTcEs C ouvtlsSIoN
428 J Street . Suite 620 . Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 . Fax (916) 322-0886
July 24,2006
David Swerdlin, Mayor
City of San Juan Capistrano
Swerdlin & Associates
31125 Via Cristal
San Juan Capistrano, CA 92675
RE: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our FiIe No.I-06-100
Dear Mayor Swerdlin:
This letter is in response to your request for assistance regarding the conflict-of-
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1
Please note that because your advice request does not refer to a specific
goveflrmental decision, we are treating your request as one for informal assistance.2
(Regulation 18329(b)(8)(D).) Commission advice is based on the facts presented to us
The Commission does not act as the finder of fact in providing advice. (In re Oglesby
(r97s) 1 FPPC Ops. 71.)
QUESTION
May you, as mayor of San Juan Capistrano, vote on a Concorde Development
project, where an individual affiliated with Concorde reviewed a pro forma report you
provided to the Califomia Coastal Commission in your role as an advocate for a different
developer?
CONCLUSION
Based on the facts in your letter, you may vote on the Concorde Development
project so long as the two development companies are not related and the review of the
pro forma report did not involve a financial transaction.
' Gorrernment Code sections 81000 - gl}l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the Califomia Code of Regulations.
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
formal written advice. (Section 831 14; regulation 18329(c)(3), copy enclosed.)


File No. I-06-100
Page No. 2
In April of this year, the California Coastal Comrnission (,,CCC,) asked you, in
your role as an advocate for Alkapuri LLC ("Alkapuri'), to deveiop u p.o for-a report on
a possible 17-room boutique hotel or bed and breakfast for a site owned by Alkapuri in
San Clemente. You researched on the Internet, called similar businesses in San
Clemente, developed a database, and wrote the pro forma report. To add credibility to
the report, the CCC asked that you confer with a developer with experience in bouiique
hotels and bed and breakfast proposals. You telephoned Mr. Dennis Gage of Concorde
Development in Newport Beach, and spe-nt 15 minutes going over the pri for*u report
with him. Mr. Gage did not charge yo, for his time. l"-u t"f"pnorr" ,ujl on June 7, 2006,
you further stated that you have not received or been promised income from Concorde
and you do not have an investment in the company.
You sent the pro forma report to the CCC on April 19, noting that Mr. Gage had
reviewed it. (The ccc minutes of May to,2oo5 (05-i-240) said thit you had
"partnered" with Mr. Gage in the report.)
- 
Dennis Gage of Concorde Development may or maynot have a project before the
San Juan Capistrano council within ayear.
FACTS
ANALYSIS
qovernment decision?
- 
The term "public official" includes every member, officer, employee or consultant
of a state or local government agency. (Section s204g.) As mayor of su, Jru,
- 
The Act prohibits a public official from makin g, participating in, or using his
official position to influence a governmental decision in which he knows or has reason to
know he has a financial interest. (Section 87100.) Section 87103 provides that a public
official has a "financial interest" in agovernmental decision "if it is."urorubty
foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishable from its
effect on the public generally, on the official, a member of his orher imlediate family,
or on any" of the official's economic interests. To determine whether a public official
has a "conflict of interest" in a govemmental decision, the Commission iras developed a
standard, eight-step analysis outlined at subdivisions I through 8 of regulation 1g700(b).
A review of this standardized analysis will assist in detennining when,-if at all, you may
face a conflict of interest.


File No. I-06-100
Page No. 3
Capistrano, you are a public official under the Act.3
3. What are )zour economic interests?
Section 87103 states that apublic official has a financial interest in a decision
when it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a mateialfinancial effect,
distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on the official or a mernber of his
or her immediate family, or on:
' 
Any business entity in which the public official has a direct or indirect
investment worth two thousand dollars ($2,000) or more. (Section
87103(a); regulation 18703.1(a)); or in which he or she is a director,
officer, partner, trustee, employee, or holds any position of management.
(Section 871 03(d); regutation I 8703. I (b));
' 
Any real property in which the public official has a direct or indirect
interest worth two thousand dollars ($2,000) or more. (Section S7103(b);
regulation 18703.2);
. Any source of income, other than gifts and other than loans by a
commercial lending instifution in the regular course of business on terms
available to the public without regardtoofficial status, aggregating five
hundred dollars ($5oo; o^r more in value provided to, .".Jii,"jby or
promised to the public official within 12 months prior to the time when the
decision is made. (section g7r03(c); reguration iszo:.:);
. Any donor of, or any intermediary or agent for a donor of; a gift or gifts
aggregating three hundred sixty dollars ($360) or more in vai-ue provided
to, received by, or promised to the public official within 12 months prior
to the time when the decision is made. (Section 87103(e); reguiation
18703.4.)
In addition to these enumerated economic interests, under section g7103 and
regulation 18703-5, a public official always has an economic interest in his or her
personal finances and those of his or her immediate family. A governmental decision
3 If a public official's office is listed in section 87200 (*g7200 filers,, include members of city
councils) and he or she has a conflict of interest in a decision noticed at a public meeting, tt 
"n ir" o. ,t "
must: (1) immediately prior to the discussion of the item, verbally identify each type oa;";;-r" interest
involved in the decision as well as details of the economic interest, as dislussed i","gurmoo 
-
18702'5(b)(l)(B), on the record of the meeting; (2) recuse himsellor herself; and (3)ieave the room for the
duration ofthe discussion andlor vote on the item. For closed sessions, consent calendars, absences and
speaking as a member of the public regarding personal intere.sts, special rules found in regulation 1g702.5,
subdivisions (c) and (d) apply (Section 8710i.) These require-*tr ur" applicable to you if you have a
conflict of interest in a governmental decision.


File No. I-06-100
Page No. 4
will have an effect on this economic interest if the decision will result in the personal
expenses, income, asset or liabilities of the official or his or her immediate family
increasing or decreasing.
As an advocate for AlkapuiT,.LC, you may have aneconomic interest in Atkapuri
LLC as a source of income. (Regulation 187CR3(a).) Your letter does not state whether
or how much you ale paid by Arkapuri LLC. How "i"r, *"need not evaluate your
economic interests in Alkapuri, since your question is limited to possible conflicts of
interest in decisions involving Concorde.
You have stated that you do not receive nor have you been promised income from
concorde, and you do not have an investment in the company. If you and concorde are
involved in a joint venture, you may have an economic interest in Concorde. The
common definition of a.Joint venture,, is:
"A special combination of two or more persons, where in some
specific velt{e, aprofit is jointly sought without any actualpartnership or
corporate designation, or as an association ofpersons to carry out a single
business enterprise for profit, -fo1 which they combine their propert%
money, effects, skill, and knowledge.- (sadugor v. Holstein 1toaz.1 too
Cal.App.2d 477,483.)
Further, the sharing ofprofits and losses in a common enterprise is usually an
indispensable feature of a joint venture. (9 witkin Summ. car.Law (rOth ed. zoo5)
Partnership Section 1 l.)
Your interaction with concorde would seem to fall short of ajoint venture even
though the ccC said you "partnered" with concorde. There is no indication that the 15
minutes Mr' Gage spent reviewing the pro forma report on the Alkapuri pG" was an
attempt to combine skills with you or Alkapuri for the goal of sharing p.ontl or losses.
From what we know of the facts, it does not appearthat would experience a
personal financial effect as a consequence ofany decision regarding coniorde.
Regulation 18705.5 prescribes the standard for assessing whether an effect on an
official's personal finances is material. Reguration tszo's.s1a) provides ttui;Jr1
reasonably foreseeable personal financial "if""t is material iritls at least $250 in any t2-
month period'" As such your economic interest in yourpersonal finances would be
materially affected by a decision regarding concorie oniy if it results in an increase or
decrease of at least $250 in the personal 
"ip"rrr.r, ir"o-r, assets, or liabilities of you or
your immediate family members in any l2_monthperiod.
Your letter states that Concorde may be initiating an action before the San Juan
capiskano city council within ayear. Because it doesiot appearfrom what we know of
the facts that you have an economic interest in concorde or any other economic interest


File No. I-06-100
Page No. 5
that might be affected by a decision on such a matter,you may vote on such actions if
there is no personal financial effect.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter,please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
q "^J2 
?^n a.t 6n cLa-6.-
J
By: Gail Maiorana
Intem, Legal Division

project, where an individual affiliated with Concorde reviewed a pro forma report you

QUESTION: May you, as mayor of San Juan Capistrano, vote on a Concorde Development
project, where an individual affiliated with Concorde reviewed a pro forma report you
provided to the Califomia Coastal Commission in your role as an advocate for a different
developer?

CONCLUSION: Based on the facts in your letter, you may vote on the Concorde Development
project so long as the two development companies are not related and the review of the
pro forma report did not involve a financial transaction.
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
formal written advice. (Section 831 14; regulation 18329(c)(3), copy enclosed.)
In April of this year, the California Coastal Comrnission (,,CCC,) asked you, in
your role as an advocate for Alkapuri LLC ("Alkapuri'), to deveiop u p.o for-a report on
a possible 17-room boutique hotel or bed and breakfast for a site owned by Alkapuri in
San Clemente. You researched on the Internet, called similar businesses in San
Clemente, developed a database, and wrote the pro forma report. To add credibility to
the report, the CCC asked that you confer with a developer with experience in bouiique
hotels and bed and breakfast proposals. You telephoned Mr. Dennis Gage of Concorde
Development in Newport Beach, and spe-nt 15 minutes going over the pri for*u report
with him. Mr. Gage did not charge yo, for his time. l"-u t"f"pnorr" ,ujl on June 7, 2006,
you further stated that you have not received or been promised income from Concorde
and you do not have an investment in the company.
You sent the pro forma report to the CCC on April 19, noting that Mr. Gage had
reviewed it. (The ccc minutes of May to,2oo5 (05-i-240) said thit you had
"partnered" with Mr. Gage in the report.)
- 
Dennis Gage of Concorde Development may or maynot have a project before the
San Juan Capistrano council within ayear.
